20	// TestParseClaudeStream feeds synthetic stream-json events through the
21	// parser and asserts:
22	//
23	//  1. Progress lines (session.start, llm.response, tool.call) are emitted
24	//     for relevant events so the existing TailSessionLogs pipeline can
25	//     render them.
26	//  2. The final aggregated result captures the text, tokens, cost, and
27	//     session id from the authoritative "result" event.
28	//  3. The parser stays lenient in the face of garbage lines.
29	//
30	// The table-driven form exercises multiple event shapes so regressions in
31	// one case don't hide breakage in another.
32	func TestParseClaudeStream(t *testing.T) {
33		cases := []struct {
34			name              string
35			input             string
36			wantTurnCount     int
37			wantToolCalls     int
38			wantInputTokens   int
39			wantOutputTokens  int
40			wantCostUSD       float64
41			wantFinalText     string
42			wantSessionID     string
43			wantModel         string
44			wantProgressTypes []string
45		}{
46			{
47				name: "full stream with tool use and result",
48				input: strings.Join([]string{
49					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-abc","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","tools":["Bash","Read"]}`,
50					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-1","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Starting"},{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-1","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"ls"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":120,"output_tokens":42}}}`,
51					`{"type":"user","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"tu-1","content":"README.md\nfoo.go"}]}}`,
52					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-2","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Done."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":260,"output_tokens":88}}}`,
53					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":1200,"result":"All done.","usage":{"input_tokens":260,"output_tokens":88},"total_cost_usd":0.0123,"session_id":"sess-abc"}`,
54				}, "\n"),
55				wantTurnCount:     2,
56				wantToolCalls:     1,
57				wantInputTokens:   260,
58				wantOutputTokens:  88,
59				wantCostUSD:       0.0123,
60				wantFinalText:     "All done.",
61				wantSessionID:     "sess-abc",
62				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
63				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response", "tool.call", "llm.response"},
64			},
65			{
66				name: "garbage lines are skipped",
67				input: strings.Join([]string{
68					`not json`,
69					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-xyz","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`,
70					`{garbage`,
71					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-2","name":"Read","input":{"path":"/tmp/x"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5}}}`,
72					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"ok","usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5},"total_cost_usd":0.001,"session_id":"sess-xyz"}`,
73				}, "\n"),
74				wantTurnCount:     1,
75				wantToolCalls:     1,
76				wantInputTokens:   10,
77				wantOutputTokens:  5,
78				wantCostUSD:       0.001,
79				wantFinalText:     "ok",
80				wantSessionID:     "sess-xyz",
81				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
82				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response", "tool.call"},
83			},
84			{
85				name: "text-only assistant without tool_use still emits progress",
86				input: strings.Join([]string{
87					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-t","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`,
88					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":2}}}`,
89					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"hello","usage":{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":2},"total_cost_usd":0.0001,"session_id":"sess-t"}`,
90				}, "\n"),
91				wantTurnCount:     1,
92				wantToolCalls:     0,
93				wantInputTokens:   3,
94				wantOutputTokens:  2,
95				wantCostUSD:       0.0001,
96				wantFinalText:     "hello",
97				wantSessionID:     "sess-t",
98				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
99				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response"},
100			},
101		}
102	
103		for _, tc := range cases {
104			tc := tc
105			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
106				var progressBuf bytes.Buffer
107				start := time.Now().Add(-3 * time.Second) // exercise elapsed_ms > 0
108				res, err := parseClaudeStream(strings.NewReader(tc.input), &progressBuf, "sess-test", "ddx-12345678", start)
109				require.NoError(t, err)
110				require.NotNil(t, res)
111	
112				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantTurnCount, res.TurnCount, "turn count")
113				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantToolCalls, res.ToolCalls, "tool call count")
114				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantInputTokens, res.InputTokens, "input tokens")
115				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantOutputTokens, res.OutputTokens, "output tokens")
116				assert.InDelta(t, tc.wantCostUSD, res.CostUSD, 1e-9, "cost usd")
117				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantFinalText, res.FinalText, "final text")
118				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantSessionID, res.SessionID, "session id")
119				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantModel, res.Model, "model")
120				assert.False(t, res.IsError)
121	
122				// Walk the emitted progress lines and confirm the expected types
123				// appear in order.
124				var gotTypes []string
125				for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(progressBuf.String()), "\n") {
126					if line == "" {
127						continue
128					}
129					var entry map[string]any
130					require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry), "progress line must be valid JSON: %s", line)
131					t, _ := entry["type"].(string)
132					gotTypes = append(gotTypes, t)
133				}
134				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantProgressTypes, gotTypes, "progress event types (in order)")
135	
136				// Spot-check that at least one tool.call line carries the bead_id
137				// so execute-bead operators can grep the log stream.
138				if tc.wantToolCalls > 0 {
139					assert.Contains(t, progressBuf.String(), `"bead_id":"ddx-12345678"`)
140					assert.Contains(t, progressBuf.String(), `"tool.call"`)
141				}
142			})
143		}
144	}

146	// TestParseClaudeStreamEmpty verifies the parser tolerates an empty stream
147	// (e.g. claude crashed before producing any events) and returns an empty but
148	// non-nil result rather than panicking.
149	func TestParseClaudeStreamEmpty(t *testing.T) {
150		res, err := parseClaudeStream(strings.NewReader(""), nil, "sess-empty", "ddx-0", time.Now())
151		require.NoError(t, err)
152		require.NotNil(t, res)
153		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.TurnCount)
154		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.InputTokens)
155		assert.Empty(t, res.FinalText)
156	}
157	
158	// TestClaudeStreamArgsUnsupported ensures the stderr-detection helper that
159	// drives fallback-to-legacy-args recognises the phrases we care about.
160	func TestClaudeStreamArgsUnsupported(t *testing.T) {
161		cases := []struct {
162			stderr string
163			want   bool
164		}{
165			{"error: unknown option '--output-format'", true},
166			{"Error: unrecognized option --verbose", true},
167			{"error: Invalid value for --output-format: stream-json", true},
168			{"Usage: claude [options]\n\nerror: unknown argument", true},
169			{"error: unknown flag: --output-format", true},
170			{"rate limit exceeded", false},
171			{"", false},
172		}
173		for _, tc := range cases {
174			assert.Equal(t, tc.want, claudeStreamArgsUnsupported(tc.stderr), tc.stderr)
175		}
176	}

178	// TestExtractUsageClaudeStreamJSON confirms the legacy extractor now also
179	// handles stream-json output (the final "type":"result" line).
180	func TestExtractUsageClaudeStreamJSON(t *testing.T) {
181		stream := strings.Join([]string{
182			`{"type":"system","subtype":"init"}`,
183			`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hi"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1}}}`,
184			`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"hi","usage":{"input_tokens":7,"output_tokens":9},"total_cost_usd":0.0042}`,
185		}, "\n")
186		usage := ExtractUsage("claude", stream)
187		assert.Equal(t, 7, usage.InputTokens)
188		assert.Equal(t, 9, usage.OutputTokens)
189		assert.InDelta(t, 0.0042, usage.CostUSD, 1e-9)
190	
191		// And the text extractor pulls the final result text.
192		assert.Equal(t, "hi", ExtractOutput("claude", stream))
193	}
194	
195	// TestResolveClaudeProgressLogDir documents the precedence rule that fixes
196	// the claude/execute-bead parity gap: the per-run override wins over the
197	// runner-wide config so execute-bead can redirect the JSONL trace into the
198	// execution bundle's embedded/ dir.
199	func TestResolveClaudeProgressLogDir(t *testing.T) {
200		cases := []struct {
201			name string
202			opts RunArgs
203			cfg  Config
204			want string
205		}{
206			{
207				name: "opts override wins over config",
208				opts: RunArgs{SessionLogDir: "/bundle/embedded"},
209				cfg:  Config{SessionLogDir: "/runner/default"},
210				want: "/bundle/embedded",
211			},
212			{
213				name: "falls back to config when opts empty",
214				opts: RunArgs{},
215				cfg:  Config{SessionLogDir: "/runner/default"},
216				want: "/runner/default",
217			},
218			{
219				name: "empty when both unset",
220				opts: RunArgs{},
221				cfg:  Config{},
222				want: "",
223			},
224			{
225				name: "opts override wins even when config empty",
226				opts: RunArgs{SessionLogDir: "/bundle/embedded"},
227				cfg:  Config{},
228				want: "/bundle/embedded",
229			},
230		}
231		for _, tc := range cases {
232			tc := tc
233			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
234				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(tc.opts, tc.cfg))
235			})
236		}
237	}

239	// writeFakeClaudeBinary creates a shell script that mimics the claude CLI's
240	// stream-json output. The script ignores all arguments and prints a minimal
241	// but complete sequence of stream events so runClaudeStreaming has real
242	// bytes to parse and the progress log file ends up with content.
243	func writeFakeClaudeBinary(t *testing.T, dir string) string {
244		t.Helper()
245		path := filepath.Join(dir, "fake-claude")
246		script := `#!/bin/sh
247	cat <<'EOF'
248	{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-fake","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}
249	{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-1","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":5,"output_tokens":2}}}
250	{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":10,"result":"hello","usage":{"input_tokens":5,"output_tokens":2},"total_cost_usd":0.0001,"session_id":"sess-fake"}
251	EOF
252	`
253		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(script), 0o755))
254		return path
255	}
256	
257	// findClaudeProgressLog locates the agent-*.jsonl trace file that
258	// runClaudeStreaming writes. Returns the path to the single matching file.
259	func findClaudeProgressLog(t *testing.T, dir string) string {
260		t.Helper()
261		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
262		require.NoError(t, err)
263		var found []string
264		for _, e := range entries {
265			if e.IsDir() {
266				continue
267			}
268			name := e.Name()
269			if strings.HasPrefix(name, "agent-") && strings.HasSuffix(name, ".jsonl") {
270				found = append(found, filepath.Join(dir, name))
271			}
272		}
273		require.Len(t, found, 1, "expected exactly one agent-*.jsonl in %s, got %v", dir, found)
274		return found[0]
275	}

277	// TestParseClaudeStreamLatencyIsPerCall feeds two assistant turns separated
278	// by a tool round-trip and asserts that latency_ms reflects per-call duration
279	// (request → response wall time), not cumulative elapsed_ms. This is the
280	// regression guard for the bug where every llm.response had
281	// `latency_ms == elapsed_ms`.
282	func TestParseClaudeStreamLatencyIsPerCall(t *testing.T) {
283		pr, pw := io.Pipe()
284	
285		// Drive the parser through a pipe with real sleeps between events so
286		// time.Now() advances and the parser computes meaningful intervals.
287		const (
288			firstCallDelay  = 80 * time.Millisecond
289			toolExecDelay   = 120 * time.Millisecond
290			secondCallDelay = 60 * time.Millisecond
291		)
292	
293		go func() {
294			defer pw.Close()
295			fmt.Fprintln(pw, `{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-lat","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`)
296			// First LLM call: takes firstCallDelay before the assistant turn arrives.
297			time.Sleep(firstCallDelay)
298			fmt.Fprintln(pw, `{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-1","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-1","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"ls"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5}}}`)
299			// Tool execution happens locally (LLM is idle).
300			time.Sleep(toolExecDelay)
301			fmt.Fprintln(pw, `{"type":"user","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"tu-1","content":"ok"}]}}`)
302			// Second LLM call begins after the user event; takes secondCallDelay.
303			time.Sleep(secondCallDelay)
304			fmt.Fprintln(pw, `{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-2","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"done"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":20,"output_tokens":3}}}`)
305			fmt.Fprintln(pw, `{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"result":"done","usage":{"input_tokens":20,"output_tokens":3},"total_cost_usd":0.001,"session_id":"sess-lat"}`)
306		}()
307	
308		var progressBuf bytes.Buffer
309		start := time.Now()
310		res, err := parseClaudeStream(pr, &progressBuf, "sess-lat", "ddx-test", start)
311		require.NoError(t, err)
312		require.NotNil(t, res)
313		require.Equal(t, 2, res.TurnCount)
314	
315		// Collect llm.response entries in order.
316		type llmResp struct {
317			LatencyMS int64 `json:"latency_ms"`
318			ElapsedMS int64 `json:"elapsed_ms"`
319			Turn      int   `json:"turn"`
320		}
321		var responses []llmResp
322		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(progressBuf.String()), "\n") {
323			if line == "" {
324				continue
325			}
326			var entry struct {
327				Type string  `json:"type"`
328				Data llmResp `json:"data"`
329			}
330			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
331			if entry.Type == "llm.response" {
332				responses = append(responses, entry.Data)
333			}
334		}
335		require.Len(t, responses, 2, "expected two llm.response events")
336	
337		// Turn 1: latency ≈ firstCallDelay; elapsed ≈ firstCallDelay too (both
338		// measured from session start). They may be equal here, which is fine.
339		turn1 := responses[0]
340		assert.Equal(t, 1, turn1.Turn)
341		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, turn1.LatencyMS, int64(50), "turn 1 latency should reflect first call duration")
342	
343		// Turn 2: latency must be the per-call duration (≈ secondCallDelay),
344		// NOT the cumulative elapsed_ms. This is the core regression assertion.
345		turn2 := responses[1]
346		assert.Equal(t, 2, turn2.Turn)
347		assert.NotEqual(t, turn2.LatencyMS, turn2.ElapsedMS,
348			"turn 2 latency_ms must not equal cumulative elapsed_ms")
349		assert.Less(t, turn2.LatencyMS, turn2.ElapsedMS,
350			"per-call latency must be strictly less than cumulative elapsed for events past turn 1")
351		// The second call latency should exclude the tool execution window.
352		assert.Less(t, turn2.LatencyMS, int64(firstCallDelay/time.Millisecond)+int64(toolExecDelay/time.Millisecond),
353			"turn 2 latency should not include prior turn + tool exec time")
354	
355		// Sum of per-call latencies must be <= elapsed_ms of the last event
356		// (intervals are disjoint within the session).
357		var sumLatency int64
358		for _, r := range responses {
359			sumLatency += r.LatencyMS
360		}
361		assert.LessOrEqual(t, sumLatency, responses[len(responses)-1].ElapsedMS,
362			"sum of per-call latencies must not exceed cumulative elapsed_ms")
363	}
364	
365	// TestRunClaudeStreaming_OptsSessionLogDirOverridesConfig exercises the
366	// claude streaming path end-to-end and confirms the per-run override
367	// (opts.SessionLogDir) wins over the runner-wide Config.SessionLogDir.
368	// This is the execute-bead parity behavior: the JSONL progress trace must
369	// land in the DDx-owned bundle dir, not the runner's default log dir.
370	func TestRunClaudeStreaming_OptsSessionLogDirOverridesConfig(t *testing.T) {
371		if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
372			t.Skip("fake claude binary relies on POSIX shell")
373		}
374		tmp := t.TempDir()
375		binPath := writeFakeClaudeBinary(t, tmp)
376		runnerDefaultDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "runner-default")
377		bundleEmbeddedDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "bundle-embedded")
378	
379		r := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: runnerDefaultDir})
380		harness := harnessConfig{Name: "claude", Binary: binPath, PromptMode: "arg"}
381		opts := RunArgs{
382			Harness:       "claude",
383			Prompt:        "hi",
384			SessionLogDir: bundleEmbeddedDir,
385		}
386	
387		result, err := runClaudeStreamingFn(r, context.Background(), harness, "claude", "", opts, "hi", "", 10*time.Second)
388		require.NoError(t, err)
389		require.NotNil(t, result)
390	
391		// Progress trace must land in the opts-provided dir, not the config dir.
392		assert.NoDirExists(t, runnerDefaultDir, "runner default dir must stay empty when opts override is set")
393		logPath := findClaudeProgressLog(t, bundleEmbeddedDir)
394		data, err := os.ReadFile(logPath)
395		require.NoError(t, err)
396		// At minimum the session.start and llm.response events should be present.
397		assert.Contains(t, string(data), `"session.start"`)
398		assert.Contains(t, string(data), `"llm.response"`)
399	}
400	
401	// TestRunClaudeStreaming_FallsBackToConfigLogDir is the regression guard:
402	// when opts.SessionLogDir is empty, the trace must still go to the runner's
403	// configured SessionLogDir (the pre-fix default behavior).
404	func TestRunClaudeStreaming_FallsBackToConfigLogDir(t *testing.T) {
405		if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
406			t.Skip("fake claude binary relies on POSIX shell")
407		}
408		tmp := t.TempDir()
409		binPath := writeFakeClaudeBinary(t, tmp)
410		runnerDefaultDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "runner-default")
411	
412		r := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: runnerDefaultDir})
413		harness := harnessConfig{Name: "claude", Binary: binPath, PromptMode: "arg"}
414		opts := RunArgs{
415			Harness: "claude",
416			Prompt:  "hi",
417			// SessionLogDir deliberately left empty — must fall back to config.
418		}
419	
420		result, err := runClaudeStreamingFn(r, context.Background(), harness, "claude", "", opts, "hi", "", 10*time.Second)
421		require.NoError(t, err)
422		require.NotNil(t, result)
423	
424		logPath := findClaudeProgressLog(t, runnerDefaultDir)
425		data, err := os.ReadFile(logPath)
426		require.NoError(t, err)
427		assert.Contains(t, string(data), `"session.start"`)
428	}

Commit landed. AC summary:
1. ✅ `DDX_AGENT_LOG_VERBOSE=1` produces enriched llm.response (content/finish_reason/usage) and tool.call (input/output/duration_ms/error) — verified by `TestParseClaudeStreamVerboseMode`.
2. ✅ Schema documented as a Go doc comment block in `cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go`.
3. ✅ Default behavior unchanged — verified by `TestParseClaudeStreamVerboseLeanDefault` and the existing `TestParseClaudeStream` table tests still passing.
4. ✅ Test fixture `TestParseClaudeStreamVerboseMode` asserts every required verbose field is populated.
